annotations.text: Severe hypertension can cause mental-status changes with psychosis, but I would expect encephalopathy with confusion and other end-organ involvement to be present.

Delirium, which is accompanied by psychosis in about 50% of cases, is unlikely in this case, because the patient’s psychiatric symptoms were neither acute nor characterized by a waxing and waning course.
Although many tests are not needed in an emergency situation, all tests should be considered when acute care is being provided, especially if subsequent outpatient follow-up is unlikely.
the main distinction to make is between schizophrenia spectrum disorders and psychotic mood disorders.
Her positive symptoms include delusions, auditory hallucinations, and a disorganized thought process.
The absence of clinically significant mood symptoms argues against a diagnosis of psychotic mood disorders.

She disputed the diagnosis of schizophrenia by showing me a passage from a spirituality book she was reading that contained this quote from Lily Tomlin: “Why is it that when we speak to God we are said to be praying but when God speaks to us we are said to be schizophrenic?”
The core symptoms — hallucinations, delusions, paranoia, diminished curiosity and motivation, and impaired executive functioning — create challenges in meeting the demands of adult life.
Sixty years ago, a patient such as this one would probably have been cared for in an institution.
Assertive community treatment by a non–office-based multidisciplinary outreach team is an established approach for doing just that.
With appropriate treatment and support, the majority of previously homeless people with schizophrenia can live stably in the community, as this patient is now doing.

In contrast to linear housing paradigms, which require a person to have sequential engagement in services and to demonstrate clinical stability before moving into independent housing, the Housing First model allows a person to move directly into independent housing, with clinical and social services available as adjuncts.
